system.description:
CLASS DICE: D20

Mastery

You start each session with Mastery. While you have it, you can add your D20 when casting spells. If you do, the dice roll includes the 20-special (activated on a 20).

20-Special: this success counts as two successes.

This is a role-playing game. Yes, you see a world around you... but you know there's rules behind it. And someone running it. Ideally, one day it's going to be you. Until then, you're creeping around the edges of the game, seeing what you can get away with.

Most people look at you and think "wizard". Pfft! Wizards cast spells. You mess with the rules to get what's possible and outright cheat when you need the impossible. The world is a game. That means it's your plaything.

Until someone notices you.

Play the Master if you want to...

  • Re-arrange rules to create your own custom magic spells.
  • Outright cheat when you need something literally impossible.
  • Beg for your life from judgemental extradimensional beings when you get caught.
system.coreNature:
This is a role-playing game. Yes, you see a world around you... but you know there's rules behind it. And someone running it. Ideally, one day it's going to be you. Until then, you're creeping around the edges of the game, seeing what you can get away with.

Most people look at you and think "wizard". Pfft! Wizards cast spells. You mess with the rules to get what's possible and outright cheat when you need the impossible. The world is a game. That means it's your plaything.

Until someone notices you.

PLAY THE MASTER IF YOU WANT TO...

  • Re-arrange rules to create your own custom magic spells.
  • Outright cheat when you need something literally impossible.
  • Beg for your life from judgemental extradimensional beings when you get caught.

coreAbility.name: MASTERY
coreAbility.description:
You start each session with Mastery. While you have it, you can add your D20 when casting spells. If you do, the dice roll includes the 20-special.

20-Special: this success counts as two successes.

looks.name: Wizard robes straight out of a 1980s kids fantasy cartoon
Killer suits straight off a 1980s corporate bad guy
Fetish-wear straight out of a 1990s vampire LARP

specials.name: 20-Special
specials.description: This success counts as two successes

fields.label: MY GAME, MY RULES
fields.description:
Select three Rules from the Rules section overleaf – elements of reality that you can manipulate with magic. You have power over these rules, and you do not suffer risk when you add them to spells that you have crafted.

To cast a spell, combine as many rules as you need to get the effect you desire. Then, calculate Risk:

  • For every rule you don't control, add 5 Risk.
  • For every effect doing slightly more than the standard rule, add 1 Risk.
  • For every effect doing massively more than the standard rule, add 5 Risk.

fields.label: Selected Rules
choices: Range - Effect reaches medium range
Area - Affect all within melee range of target
Duration - Lasts for an encounter
Selectivity - Remove yourself from spell effects
Component (Negative) - Requires rare thematic component
Blood Cost (Negative) - Causes 1 Wound to caster or target
Taint of Damnation (Negative) - Disadvantage on social checks
Secrets (Negative) - Must share meaningful secret
Damage: Duration - Special: ignite, 1 hit/round
Damage: Debilitate - Special: Disadvantage on next action
Damage: Paralyse - Special: immobilize if Willpower ≤4
Damage: Chain - Special: hit another target nearby
Damage: Curse - Special: Disadvantage on next action
Damage: Barrage - Double Special: inflict 2 hits
Damage: Bane - Special: 2 hits vs chosen creature type
Damage: Precise - Special: bypass Guard
Heal - Heal 1 Health. Special: +1 Health
Invisibility - Target invisible until hostile action
Counterspell - Cancel ongoing magical effect
Enhance - Add Advantage on defined task/stat
Hinder - Add Disadvantage on defined task/stat
Illusion - Object appears as something else
Manipulate Mechanism - Single mechanism, single action
Detect - Notice presence of a noun (magic, death, etc)
Create Wall - Object with 2×successes Health, 1 hour
Create Obstacle - Difficult terrain, stat roll to traverse
Sleep - Special: sleep if Willpower ≤4
Understand - Comprehend a noun (language, animal, dead)
Create Creature - Summon being (2/2/4/0 stats)
Create Intelligence - Add sapient mind to creation
Exchange - Move 1 stat point between two stats
Intangibility - Move through objects
Levitation - Move freely in all directions
Speed - Move at horse speed
Telepathy - Share thoughts/vision with caster

fields.description:
If a spell has more than 20 Risk, it cannot be cast. Otherwise, if the spell has any Risk, roll an Int pool including your Mastery D20.

  • If the D20 result is greater than the Risk, you suffer no consequences for casting the spell.
  • If the D20 result is equal to or less than the Risk, the Master is caught in their Rules Lawyering by the Powers That Be. They lose their Mastery, and they can no longer add a D20 to rolls. If they've already lost Mastery this session, the Powers That Be deliver punishment – bodily harm, vile curses, ironic undoings, instant death, and worse. See overleaf for more details.

fields.label: HARDCORE CHEATING
fields.description: Rules can be bent, stretched and manipulated into achieving all kinds of effects, but there's a greater magic at your control. You can break the laws of the universe entirely; you can cheat. But such blatant disregard for reality comes with a steep cost if discovered by the Powers That Be.
Every Master has a style or a preference that determines the kind of cheats they can perform. What's yours?
choices: Gamist - Mess with rules in this book (e.g., players don't die at 0 health)
Simulationist - Mess with physical laws (e.g., shift gravity, summon storms)
Narrativist - Mess with the story (e.g., right stranger at right time)

fields.initial:
To cheat, say what you want to do and the GM will describe a punishment if you're caught. If you wish to continue, take two containers (e.g. your hands) and secretly hide the D20 in one of them. The GM guesses which container the D20 is in:

  • If they miss the D20, you get away with it and the effect happens.
  • If they find it, the Powers That Be notice you. A trial will begin.

A Trial is a short role-played scene. The Powers That Be appear before you, fully aware of the bad thing you've done. Prepare to beg for your life, make excuses or justify yourself quickly. Good luck.

fields.label: New Rule
fields.description: Your understanding of the fundamental forces that underpin reality grows ever-deeper. Pick a new rule.

fields.label: Reading the Rules
fields.description:
When you enter an environment that is under the power of another Master (or similar reality-controlling force), you're aware of it.

  • You may roll an Int pool to ask the GM one question per success about the nature of the realm you're in.
  • You may roll an Wis pool to ask the GM one question per success about the nature of the Master.

A fail alerts the Master to your presence. On a critical fail you hit a trap: the Master may attack you with a spell of their choice.

fields.label: Slow and Steady
fields.description: Between adventures, you may perform a Hardcore Cheat without being caught. This cannot be at the Outrageous level.

fields.label: Further Mastery
fields.description: Pick one of these abilities, or gain a New Rule.

fields.label: A Great Rule
fields.hint: Which rules can you push a little without Risk?
fields.description: Choose one of your Rules. You can now use it as "slightly more than the rule" as if it were the normal definition of the rule – you can push it a little without gaining Risk.

fields.label: Extra Mastery (Pick Once)
fields.description: You have two stacks of Mastery, and so can lose Mastery once and still retain your Mastery abilities and ability to cast spells with Risk. Both stacks refresh at the start of each session.

fields.label: 2nd Multimaster Domain
fields.description: You shake off the petty fetters of GNS theory. The first time you take this, pick a second Master type – you may now perform Minor cheats in that domain.
choices: Gamist - Mess with rules in this book (e.g., players don't die at 0 health)
Simulationist - Mess with physical laws (e.g., shift gravity, summon storms)
Narrativist - Mess with the story (e.g., right stranger at right time)

fields.label: 3rd Multimaster Domain
fields.description: If you take this again, raise the tier of cheats you can perform in that domain by one, or unlock a third domain starting at Minor.
choices: Gamist - Mess with rules in this book (e.g., players don't die at 0 health)
Simulationist - Mess with physical laws (e.g., shift gravity, summon storms)
Narrativist - Mess with the story (e.g., right stranger at right time)

fields.label: A Dungeon of One's Own
fields.description: The Master creates their own dungeon in a pocket dimension.

fields.label: Choose how it's accessed:
choices: Via a real doorway. | Pro: no-one can enter unless invited. Con: fixed in place.
Via a small magical object (lamp, coin). | Pro: Portable. Con: Can be taken from you.
Via a series of magical words or methods. | Pro, Con: Can be accessed by anyone who knows the method.
fields.description: You have Mastery over the dungeon, and can use Realm Master cheats within it. You are free to design the layout of the dungeon, with a number of rooms up to your level. It takes up no more space than can fit on a physical sheet of graph paper, with each square forming a 10 foot cube. You can make a digital version of your dungeon, but you must have a piece of physical graph paper to show how big it can be. Every time you go up a level you can add a room, and between adventures you can delete a room and draw up to your level total.

fields.label: Realm Mastery
fields.description: You may now become Master of a region of Die. If an area has no Master, simply claim it. If it already has a Master, you must force them to surrender it. You can expand your dominion indefinitely if you're able to claim more terrains. Within your region, you gain access to the Realm Master Hardcore Cheats. Also, pick a FURTHER MASTERY ability.

fields.label: Further Mastery Ability
choices: New Rule
A Great Rule
Extra Mastery
Multimaster

fields.label: To the Dungeon!
fields.description: You may teleport anywhere in any Realm you have mastery of, or a Realm whose Master has given you permission.

fields.label: Define what your teleportation looks like.
fields.hint: You can bring a party-worth of people with you.

fields.label: Writing the Rules
fields.description: These abilities fundamentally change the world. To take them, you must be a Realm Master (either from Realm Mastery or a Dungeon of One's Own), and should work the nature of your Realm into whatever you create.

fields.label: The Handbook (Writing the Rules)
fields.description:
Work with the GM to add a whole new sort of adventurer to the world. What do they do? What is their reputation? To whom do they owe allegiance? And, most importantly, why are they thought of as heroic?

Roll your Int pool with the D20: their greatest band of heroes has one member per success. They can be sent off to perform missions as per the Compressed Adventure Rules (p. 279).

fields.label: To whom do they owe allegiance?
Why are they thought of as heroic?
The Manual (Writing the Rules)
fields.description:
Work with the GM to add a whole new monstrous species to the world. What do they look like? What do they want? To whom do they owe allegiance? And, most importantly, why are they thought of as monsters?

If there's an empty region in the map of DIE, this is their homeland. If not, they emerge into your realm. They are a Great Power. Roll your Int pool with the D20: there is one major faction within this Great Power per success. Define them.

fields.label: To whom do they owe allegiance?
Why are they thought of as monsters?
The Guide (Writing the Rules)
fields.description:
Work with the GM to add a whole new realm to this world. What is it known for? What are the landmarks? What can be found there? And most importantly, why do people never go there?

Select an empty region on the map of Die to be its home. Roll your Int pool with the D20: for each success, define one thing which can be found in this realm. When you define it, also define what prevents people taking it.

fields.label: Why do people never go there?
Things Found There (per success)
fields.hint: List each treasure and what prevents taking it.

fields.label: The Arcana (Writing the Rules)
fields.description:
Work with the GM to add a whole new form of magic to the world. What is this source of power? How do people access it? What can it do? Does it have any iconic magical creatures? And, most importantly, what has prevented its discovery until now?

If you wish, work with the GM to create a new Rule to represent this new form of magic. You now possess this rule. Roll your Int pool with the D20: select one extra Rule per success. Your Risk thresholds on these rules are one step down from normal: "massively more than the standard rule" adds 1 Risk, "slightly more than the standard rule" is free.

fields.label: What is this source of power?
What prevented its discovery until now?
AT LEVEL 20: GRANDMASTER
fields.description:
DIE advancement assumes to end a campaign well before the Paragons reach level 20... but if you do, this is the monster lurking there for a Master. When you reach level 20, you become all powerful for exactly one second. What do you do?

You are then consumed by DIE, forever.
